summary:
Effective antiretroviral therapy, especially with newer agents like TAF, enables safe kidney transplantation in select HIV-positive patients with end-stage renal disease. Guidelines do not support universal exclusions, emphasizing evaluation rather than automatic rejection based on HIV infection alone.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to two-sentence format focusing only on clinically significant findings.)
